title: Wellesley man pleads not guilty to stabbing his father to death at playground
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: James Floyd Bennett III pleads not guilty for first-degree murder of his father at a Wellesley playground.
Police responded to Phillips Park following a 911 call; victim James Floyd Bennett Jr. died from multiple stab wounds.
Bennett III's attorney cited significant mental health issues; next court date set for July 10.

title: Young boy hospitalized after falling from second-floor window in Worcester
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 4-year-old boy fell from a second-story window on Southold Road in Worcester and was hospitalized.
Worcester Police reported the incident happened at 5 p.m. Thursday, and the boy is now in stable condition.
The boy suffered bumps and bruises, but is expected to recover as the investigation into the fall continues.

title: 12-year-old dead, another child injured after bus collides with motorized scooter in Haverhill
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 12-year-old girl died and another child was seriously injured after a bus collision on a motorized scooter in Haverhill.
The incident occurred at the intersection of Portland Street and 5th Avenue, with police receiving the report shortly before 4 p.m.
Haverhill Police and State Police are investigating, and witnesses are urged to contact the department with information.

title: 4 takeaways from the U.S. loss against Türkiye in the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The United States lost to Türkiye 3-2 in their final group stage match of the 2026 World Cup.
Despite the loss, the U.S. won Group D and will face Bosnia and Herzegovina in the knockout stage.
Matt Turner started in goal for the U.S. as several regulars were rested with the group top spot secure.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com released a week-ending news quiz covering topics like Mike Wankum's departure and Tall Ship visits.
The Greater Boston News Quiz encourages locals to test their knowledge of recent regional news events.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor, invites readers to stay updated through the weekly quiz.

title: Moulton allegedly knocks phone from questioner’s hand after being asked about Graham Platner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rep. Seth Moulton allegedly knocked a phone from a person's hand at a campaign event, sparking controversy.
Moulton's campaign claims the individual was a 'paid political tracker' from America Rising, not a reporter.
Fox News denied paying the tracker, stating it never hires trackers to attend events and initially misidentified the person.

title: Durbin homers to break tie in the Red Sox’s 6-3 win over the Yankees
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Caleb Durbin hit a two-run homer in the fifth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 6-3 win over the Yankees.
Connelly Early allowed two runs over six innings, striking out a season-high nine for the Red Sox victory.
Aroldis Chapman secured his 15th save as the Red Sox beat the Yankees at Fenway Park for the first time this season.

title: 3 injured when car crashes into Newburyport restaurant
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A BMW X3 crashed into the Black Cow restaurant in Newburyport, injuring three customers on Thursday evening.
The 67-year-old driver from Groveland was uninjured and alcohol is not suspected to be a factor.
The building inspector found no structural damage; the restaurant remains open with repairs needed in the bar area.

title: Suspect in custody after man, 58, fatally stabbed in Wellesley park
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 26-year-old man was arrested after a stabbing death in Phillips Park, Wellesley, Thursday night.
The victim, a 58-year-old man, was found with a stab wound and later died at Newton Wellesley Hospital.
The suspect, James Floyd Bennett III, is set to be arraigned in Dedham District Court; the investigation continues.

title: Framingham police will not renew Flock Safety contract after months of resident opposition
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Framingham Police will not renew their contract with Flock Safety due to privacy and surveillance concerns from residents.
The city's access to Flock's automated license plate reader system will end, and cameras will be decommissioned on June 30.
Framingham joins other Massachusetts communities like Cambridge and Natick in discontinuing Flock contracts over data sharing concerns.
